source.notes: Shared byte-exact artifact baseline for the golden container-e2e oracle. The committed valid-minimal.expected.json equals serde_json::to_vec(&artifact)+\n and is consumed BOTH by the #[ignore] parser-worker container e2e (S3 bytes) and by the fast in-process parser-core golden_artifact_bytes.rs, sharing tests/common/golden_identity.rs pinned identity constants so parser drift fails without containers.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Pins the exact artifact bytes the worker writes to S3 for an ordinary minimal replay; any byte drift in the parser-contract artifact shape fails the golden compare immediately.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Guarantees server-2 keeps consuming the same artifact bytes through the behavior-preserving v1.1 refactor; no contract change is introduced.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; downstream public stats still flow through server-2 APIs over the unchanged artifact.

source.notes: Focused fixture representing an ordinary selected sg replay seed from Phase 1 without committing corpus payloads. Phase 5.2 minimal default assertions target players with nested player-authored kills, destroyed_vehicles, and absence of default debug sidecar detail.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Proves normal local parser artifacts remain deterministic and populated from observed OCAP top-level fields while using minimal players with nested player-authored kills and destroyed_vehicles instead of full top-level entities/events.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Provides a minimal success artifact shape server-2 can later persist or compare through its own tables.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; downstream public stats still flow through server-2 APIs.

source.notes: Phase 1 malformed corpus evidence represented by a tiny invalid JSON fixture. Phase 5.1 keeps malformed input on the same compact envelope with structured failure details.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Malformed bytes produce a structured failed ParseArtifact instead of ordinary stats.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Supports parse.failed handling without implying replay discovery or persistence ownership here.
cross_app_impact.web: No public stats should be rendered from malformed input.

source.notes: Compact fixture for known OCAP top-level field drift summarized by Phase 1 shape profiling.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Recoverable schema drift stays visible through diagnostics and explicit unknowns.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Lets downstream persistence distinguish partial artifacts from hard parser failures.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; public display can later hide or label incomplete fields through server-2.

source.notes: Phase 4 raw killed-event tuple fixture preserves legacy array event shapes without copying real replay files.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Keeps old OCAP tuple compatibility evidence auditable through source refs.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Comparison harness can classify old-shape tuple behavior without changing server data ownership.

source.notes: Focused Phase 4 fixture with explicit winner and commander candidate evidence.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Proves known winner and commander candidate facts are source-backed parser evidence.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Supports later server-owned commander and outcome persistence decisions.
cross_app_impact.web: UI-visible commander/outcome display remains downstream of server-2 APIs.

source.notes: Normal compact fixture intentionally omits winner data to prove missing outcome remains explicit unknown.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Missing winner is represented as explicit unknown, not false negative evidence.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Downstream correction or inference remains server-owned.

source.notes: Focused Phase 4 vehicle-context fixture now covering ordinary vehicle kills, kills from vehicle, and destroyed vehicle rows.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Provides ordinary destroyed vehicle rows plus vehicleKills and killsFromVehicle counters without retired score weights.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Any future cross-replay score remains a separate server or harness responsibility.

source.notes: Focused aggregate fixture where same-side combat stays auditable but excluded from bounty inputs.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Teamkills remain source-backed combat events and aggregate evidence.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Bounty award calculation can consume only eligible parser inputs.
cross_app_impact.web: Public bounty display remains downstream of server-2.

source.notes: Focused Phase 4 commander-side candidate fixture with source refs and confidence.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Commander-side evidence is candidate-level observed identity only.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Canonical commander truth and moderation remain server-owned.

source.notes: Focused Phase 4 combat fixture covering explicit null-killer deaths.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Null-killer deaths stay explicit and auditable instead of becoming enemy kills.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Bounty and relationship calculation can exclude null-killer evidence.

source.notes: Focused Phase 3 compatibility fixture preserving duplicate same-name slot hints without entity merging.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Observed entities remain separate while legacy compatibility hints are explicit.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Canonical identity and merge decisions remain server-owned.

source.notes: Focused Phase 3 compatibility fixture for legacy connected-player name backfill.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Backfilled observed names are marked inferred with compatibility provenance.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Canonical matching remains server-owned despite compatibility hints.

source.notes: committed-gzipped-real-corpus: small (~10 KB raw) byte-exact REAL OCAP replay from Timeweb S3 sg-replays raw/sha256/<hash>.ocap, sha256-verified, pulled 2026-06-18. Gzipped at rest, gunzipped at runtime; baseline real-small-success.expected.json is the exact worker output (public_parse_replay -> serde_json::to_vec + trailing newline) consumed byte-for-byte by BOTH the #[ignore] parser-worker container e2e (S3 bytes) and the fast in-process golden_artifact_bytes.rs, sharing tests/common/golden_identity.rs pinned identity (replay_id=sha, source_file=raw/sha256/<hash>.ocap, checksum=real sha256).

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Pins the exact artifact bytes the worker writes to S3 for a small real production replay; any byte drift in the parser-contract artifact shape fails the golden compare immediately on real data.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Guarantees server-2 keeps consuming identical artifact bytes for real replays through the behavior-preserving v1.1 refactor; no contract change is introduced.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; downstream public stats still flow through server-2 APIs over the unchanged artifact.

source.notes: committed-gzipped-real-corpus: mid (~167 KB raw) byte-exact REAL OCAP replay from Timeweb S3 sg-replays raw/sha256/<hash>.ocap, sha256-verified, pulled 2026-06-18. Gzipped at rest, gunzipped at runtime; baseline real-mid-success.expected.json is the exact worker output consumed byte-for-byte by BOTH the container e2e (S3 bytes) and the fast in-process golden_artifact_bytes.rs, sharing the pinned golden identity.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Pins the exact artifact bytes for a mid-size real replay; catches aggregation/ordering drift the tiny fixtures miss.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Guarantees server-2 keeps consuming identical artifact bytes for real replays through the v1.1 refactor; no contract change.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; public stats flow through server-2 APIs over the unchanged artifact.

source.notes: committed-gzipped-real-corpus: LARGE-entity (~1.07 MB raw) byte-exact REAL OCAP replay from Timeweb S3 sg-replays raw/sha256/<hash>.ocap, sha256-verified, pulled 2026-06-18. The high-entity case that catches allocation/ordering drift the small fixtures miss. Gzipped at rest, gunzipped at runtime; baseline real-large-success.expected.json is the exact worker output consumed byte-for-byte by BOTH the container e2e (S3 bytes) and the fast in-process golden_artifact_bytes.rs, sharing the pinned golden identity.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Pins the exact artifact bytes for a large high-entity real replay; the strongest guard against aggregation/ordering/allocation drift in the parser core.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Guarantees server-2 keeps consuming identical artifact bytes for large real replays through the v1.1 refactor; no contract change.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; public stats flow through server-2 APIs over the unchanged artifact.

source.notes: committed-gzipped-real-corpus: REAL (~273 KB raw) OCAP replay exhibiting real schema-drift -> status=partial, from Timeweb S3 sg-replays raw/sha256/<hash>.ocap, sha256-verified, pulled 2026-06-18. The committed baseline real-partial-schema-drift.expected.json carries status=partial AND its diagnostics, so the byte-exact compare proves the partial path and its diagnostics survive. Gzipped at rest, gunzipped at runtime; consumed byte-for-byte by BOTH the container e2e (S3 bytes) and the fast in-process golden_artifact_bytes.rs, sharing the pinned golden identity.

cross_app_impact.parser_artifact: Pins the exact partial artifact bytes (status=partial + diagnostics) for a real schema-drift replay; any drift in how partials or diagnostics serialize fails the golden compare immediately.
cross_app_impact.server_2: Lets server-2 keep distinguishing partial artifacts from hard failures on real data through the v1.1 refactor; no contract change.
cross_app_impact.web: No direct UI behavior; public display can later hide or label incomplete fields through server-2.
